description: Maps Firecrawl MCP response fields to shared layer fields. Source paths use dot notation into the schema.json data payload. All values are real scraped web data — never estimates.

field_mappings.transform_note: Each extractedData entry maps to a competitor object. Fields mapped: appName → name, features → features, pricing → pricing, targetAudience → targetAudience, keyDifferentiators → differentiators. If competitor already exists (matched by sourceUrl), overwrite. If new, append.
Store the structuredMarkdown and metadata for each scraped URL. Useful for /research to re-analyze without re-scraping. Cap at 20 most recent entries.
Populate the competitor feature matrix with extracted feature lists. Used by /pm-workflow and /research to identify feature gaps and parity opportunities.

validation_rules.description: Numeric fields are considered consistent if within 5% of the existing shared layer value. Categorical and text fields must match exactly. New fields (null → value) are always consistent. Measured data superseding estimated data is always consistent. Note: scraped web content is inherently variable — text field consistency checks are best-effort.
